page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.48xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 physical cores operating at 2.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this arm64-architecture server features 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware highlight is the integrated 120,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, coupled with a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 21,344.09 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 410.26 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10%. Database operations, Redis SET operations at 356.82 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 164.41 GB/sec also rank in the top tier. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50% average tier.

Given its massive local NVMe storage capacity and high memory density, the i8ge.48xlarge offers high resource density for storage-heavy workloads without requiring external block storage attachments. This makes it highly cost-efficient for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput Redis caching layers, and intensive data-processing pipelines. The instance is optimally suited for distributed databases, high-volume static web serving, and software compilation tasks, though it is less suited for single-threaded applications or low-latency LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.48xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 cores running at 2.7 GHz on an arm64 architecture. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM and an integrated 120,000 GB local NVMe SSD storage array, supported by a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU execution, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database and Redis SET operations. While single-core performance remains average, its massive local storage and high memory-to-core ratio deliver excellent resource density for data-intensive applications. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput caching, and intensive software compilation workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.48xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server with 192 Graviton cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 120 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.48xlarge features 192 Graviton cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 120 TB local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 storage instance featuring one hundred and twenty terabytes of local NVMe SSD and top-tier multi-core performance.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 cores on arm64 architecture
120,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
1536.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier read and write bandwidth
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET, and multi-core processing
Best suited for large-scale distributed databases, caching layers, and data-intensive workloads
